trend: Agentic AI for Financial Crime Detection

sources.title: Agentic AI Drives Exponential Growth in Fraud Detection Market, Forecast to Reach $55.66 Billion by 2030
claims: Agentic AI in fraud detection: $7.73B (2025) → $11.53B (2026), CAGR 49.1%
Market forecast: $55.66B by 2030
Growth driven by digitization of payments, deepfake fraud, and agentic AI deployment

sources.title: FIS Brings Agentic AI to Banking with Anthropic, Starting with Financial Crimes
claims: FIS + Anthropic launch Financial Crimes AI Agent powered by Claude
BMO and Amalgamated Bank are pilot customers
General availability planned H2 2026
US financial institutions spend $35–40B annually on AML operations
Agent compresses AML case investigation from days to minutes

sources.title: Financial Crime Has Crossed $4.4 Trillion – AI Can Level the Playing Field
claims: Global illicit financial activity: $4.4 trillion (2025)
Increase of $1.3T since 2023 — 19.2% CAGR
Bank fraud + scam losses: $579.4B in 2025
70% of financial institutions plan to increase AI/ML investment in 2 years

sources.title: NICE Actimize X-Sight ActOne Platform Redefines Financial Crime Investigations with Agentic AI
claims: X-Sight ActOne launched April 2025
Agentic AI reduces AML investigation time by 50%+
Merges ML, NLP, and generative AI for automated evidence gathering and SAR drafting
Deployed at 650+ financial institutions

sources.title: Sardine AI Raises $70M to Make Fraud and Compliance Teams More Productive
claims: $70M Series C raised (February 2025)
$145M total funding
130% YoY ARR growth
Device intelligence + AML + sanctions screening in one platform

sources.title: Can AI close the FinCrime investigation gap? Tangos bets $20m
claims: Tangos AI raises $20M seed (July 2026)
Focus: autonomous AI for financial crime investigations at mid-market banks
Most recent major funding event in the category

sources.title: EU AI Act 2026 FAQs: What Fraud and AML Teams Need to Know
claims: EU AI Act classifies AML and fraud detection as 'high-risk AI'
Requirements: technical documentation, bias testing, audit logs, human oversight
Fines up to €35M or 7% of global annual revenue for violations
Banks must retroactively document pre-Act ML fraud models

sources.title: AI-Powered Fraud Detection Market Valued at USD 49.52 Billion by 2034
claims: AI-Powered Fraud Detection Market: $6.84B (2025) → $49.52B by 2034
North America: 44.8% market share
Key players: NICE Actimize, Featurespace, BioCatch, FICO, SAS, Quantexa, Sardine

sources.title: Fraud Detection and Prevention Market Report, 2026–2033
claims: Fraud detection & prevention market: $40.4B (2026) → $129.4B by 2033
CAGR 18.1%
Top 5 vendors hold 60%+ of market revenue

sources.title: How agentic AI is reshaping financial crime compliance
claims: Agentic AI can reduce total investigation time by up to 60%
Automates data gathering, transaction analysis, and SAR summarisation
Nearly 75% of anti-financial crime professionals plan to increase AI use

sources.title: Identity fraud trends in 2026: AI deepfakes, synthetic identities and the new pressure on digital onboarding
claims: Synthetic identity document fraud up 311% in Q1 2024 to Q1 2025
98% of businesses plan to adopt AI for financial crime screening by 2026
UK financial services compliance spend: £38.3B/yr, up 12%
Global synthetic identity fraud losses: $20–40B annually

unverified_claims: Quantexa valuation ~$800M — based on Series E press reports; exact figure not confirmed in public filings
Hawk AI serving 70+ banks — from company marketing materials, not independently audited
BioCatch $1.3B acquisition price by Permira — widely reported but not officially confirmed in regulatory filings
